memoryArtifacts.headline: Sir John Savile - Stained Glass Window
memoryArtifacts.description: "In 1470, when the chancel of Wakefield parish church was rebuilt, Sir John filled the east window with painted glass; on the right side was 'knight kneeling in his coate, a Saviles armes on his brest …

memoryArtifacts.headline: Death of Sir John Savile of Thornhill
memoryArtifacts.description: "He had a great funeral, and was carried through Wakefield and sumptuously buried at Thornhill."  The Yorkshire Archaeological Journal, Volume 13, pg 178

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir John Saville於大約1410年出生在英國英格蘭越可爾恩爾時，他的父親Sir Thomas Savile30歲，母親Margaret Pilkington28歲。他和Anne Gascoigne至少有6個兒子和5個女兒。他去世於1482年6月21日享年73歲，埋葬在英國越可爾衛克菲德。

nameMeanings.name: Saville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin; Yorkshire): habitational name from a place in northern France of which the identity is not clear, though it is probably Sainville in Eure-et-Loire, so called from Old French saisne ‘Saxon’ + ville ‘settlement’. Compare Savel , Savell , and Savelle .

variants.name: Savel
meanings: Slovak and Czech (Šavel): from the Biblical personal name Šavel, Hebrew Shaul (see Saul ). It may be a nickname for a godless person, from the archaic word šavel, which is derived from the Biblical name.

Slovenian (Šavel): probably from an old spelling of the Biblical personal name Savel (see Saul , compare 2 above). Alternatively, perhaps a nickname or a topographic name from an old vernacular spelling of the same plant name as in 4 below.
Americanized form of Slovenian Šavelj: probably a nickname or a topographic name from the plant name š(č)avelj ‘sorrel (Rumex acetosa)’.

variants.name: Savelle
meanings: English: variant of   Saville  . This form of the surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Saffell
meanings: English (Essex): from the Middle English personal name Safugel (Old Norse sǽfogl ‘sea-bird, especially the cormorant’), not recorded as a personal name in Scandinavia, but common in England after the Norman Conquest. In some cases with voicing of intervocalic /f/ to /v/, there may have been confusion with Saville .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir John Saville was born about 1410, in Thornhill, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Sir Thomas Savile, was 30 and his mother, Margaret Pilkington, was 28. He had at least 6 sons and 5 daughters with Anne Gascoigne. He died on 21 June 1482, at the age of 73, and was buried in Wakefield, Yorkshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir John Saville est né environ 1410, à Thornhill, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Sir Thomas Savile, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Margaret Pilkington, avait 28 ans. Il a eu au moins 6 fils et 5 filles avec Anne Gascoigne. Il est décédé le 21 juin 1482, à l’âge de 73 ans, et a été enterré à Wakefield, Everwyck, Angleterre.

ko.lifeSummary: Sir John Saville(이)가 대략 1410년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 토른힐에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas Savile(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret Pilkington(은)는 28세였다. 그는 Anne Gascoigne(와)과 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1482년 6월 21일 73세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 요르크실르 웨이크필드에 묻혔다.
